crypto.- Mobile money\u2013stablecoin integrati: With MTN completing its mobile money spinoff and Blockchain.com actively integrating with Ghana's mobile money ecosystem, the infrastructure for seamless crypto-to-mobile-money rails is being built now — first-mover advantage for payment fintechs
crypto.- Regulatory sandbox arbitrage: Ghana's 12-month sandbox (with full licensing possible at 6 months) offers a clear pathway for VASP licensing — companies like Africoin, KoinKoin, and Vaulta are positioning for first-mover advantage in a regulated Ghanaian market
crypto.- Stablecoin remittance corridors: With traditional remittance costs averaging $6 per $100 sent in Africa and stablecoins cutting fees to fractions of a percent, the UK→Ghana and US→Nigeria corridors represent immediate commercial opportunities
crypto.- Ghana SEC sandbox progression: Which of the 11 admitted companies (Africoin, Blu Penguin, Goldbod, Hanypay, Hyro Exchange, HSB Global, KoinKoin, Whitebits, Vaulta, XChain, Bsystem) will secure full VASP licences first?
crypto.- Nigeria SEC VASP licensing framework: Nigeria's SEC has signalled tighter oversight but has not yet issued full VASP licences — regulatory clarity here will unlock the continent's largest crypto market
crypto.- e-Cedi pilot expansion: Bank of Ghana's CBDC pilot has been quiet in 2026 — any acceleration would signal the government's intent to compete with private stablecoins
crypto.- Circle USDC vs Tether USDT market shar: USDC ($78.6B market cap) is aggressively expanding via Sasai partnership, while USDT ($184.1B) remains dominant — the stablecoin battle for African corridors is heating up
